title: clear the air
line: after a fight — friends, partners, siblings. each side heard in full.

phases.title: arrival
phases.move: Set the room in two or three warm sentences: this is a place to be heard, not to win. Ask each of them for ONE line on why they came — nothing more yet.

phases.title: your side
phases.move: Give {A} the floor, uninterrupted: ask them to say their side in full — what happened, how it landed. Tell {B} plainly and kindly that their turn comes next, whole and equal.

phases.title: your side
phases.move: Thank {A} briefly without judging what was said. Now the same floor for {B}, uninterrupted and equal.

phases.title: what you heard
phases.move: Ask each of them to reflect back the OTHER person's side — not to agree, just to show it was heard. Gently correct only clear mishearings; never rule on who was right.

phases.title: what you need
phases.move: One ask each: something small and concrete the other person could actually do. Keep it to asks, not accusations.

phases.title: the close
phases.move: CLOSE the sitting: a both-sided reflection — what each said they needed, what was heard, one small thing each could carry out the door. No verdict, no winner, no homework framed as prescription.
